headline: A US export-control directive forced Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 within days of release, dominating the month's coverage.
body: Multiple channels report that Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 were shut down by US government order shortly after public availability, with the withdrawal tied to export controls, jailbreak concerns, and EU access questions. Alongside the policy story, an inference-speed race surfaced: Xiaomi and TileRT's MiMo V2.5 Pro UltraSpeed and Google DeepMind's DiffusionGemma both claim over 1,000 tokens per second through quantization, speculative decoding, and diffusion-based text generation. Separately, agent tooling broke out around MCP, design systems, knowledge graphs, and Obsidian-backed memory, with IBM bridging design systems to code and a self-improving Hermes knowledge base built on Karpathy's LLM wiki principles. Web UI, recursive self-improvement, program synthesis, and long-context all led or broke out among tools this window.

cited_items.title: Bridging Design Systems and Code with MCP and AI Agents
